root_text.sn46.76:1.1: “Nirodhasaññā, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
root_text.sn46.76:1.2: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, nirodhasaññā kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā?
root_text.sn46.76:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nirodhasaññāsahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti …pe…
root_text.sn46.76:1.4: nirodhasaññāsahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.76:1.5: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, nirodhasaññā evaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsāti.
root_text.sn46.76:2.1: Nirodhasaññāya, bhikkhave, bhāvitāya bahulīkatāya dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—
root_text.sn46.76:2.2: diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitā.
root_text.sn46.76:2.3: Kathaṁ bhāvitāya, bhikkhave, nirodhasaññāya kathaṁ bahulīkatāya dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—
root_text.sn46.76:2.4: diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitā?
root_text.sn46.76:2.5: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nirodhasaññāsahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti …pe…
root_text.sn46.76:2.6: nirodhasaññāsahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.76:2.7: Evaṁ bhāvitāya kho, bhikkhave, nirodhasaññāya evaṁ bahulīkatāya dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—
root_text.sn46.76:2.8: diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitāti.
root_text.sn46.76:3.1: Nirodhasaññā, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahato atthāya saṁvattati, mahato yogakkhemāya saṁvattati, mahato saṁvegāya saṁvattati, mahato phāsuvihārāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn46.76:3.2: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, nirodhasaññā kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahato atthāya saṁvattati, mahato yogakkhemāya saṁvattati, mahato saṁvegāya saṁvattati, mahato phāsuvihārāya saṁvattati?
root_text.sn46.76:3.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nirodhasaññāsahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti …pe…
root_text.sn46.76:3.4: nirodhasaññāsahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.76:3.5: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, nirodhasaññā evaṁ bahulīkatā mahato atthāya saṁvattati, mahato yogakkhemāya saṁvattati, mahato saṁvegāya saṁvattati, mahato phāsuvihārāya saṁvattatī”ti.

translation_text.sn46.76:1.1: “Mendicants, when the perception of cessation is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial.
translation_text.sn46.76:1.3: It’s when a mendicant develops the perception of cessation together with the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.76:1.4: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.76:1.5: That’s how, when the perception of cessation is developed and cultivated, it’s very fruitful and beneficial.
translation_text.sn46.76:2.1: When the perception of cessation is developed and cultivated you can expect one of two results:
translation_text.sn46.76:2.2: enlightenment in this very life, or if there’s something left over, non-return.
translation_text.sn46.76:2.5: It’s when a mendicant develops the perception of cessation together with the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.76:2.6: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.76:2.7: When the perception of cessation is developed and cultivated in this way you can expect one of two results:
translation_text.sn46.76:2.8: enlightenment in this very life, or if there’s something left over, non-return.”
translation_text.sn46.76:3.1: “The perception of cessation, when developed and cultivated, leads to great benefit … great sanctuary from the yoke … great inspiration … great ease.
translation_text.sn46.76:3.3: It’s when a mendicant develops the perception of cessation together with the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.76:3.4: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.76:3.5: That’s how the perception of cessation, when developed and cultivated, leads to great benefit … great sanctuary from the yoke … great inspiration … great ease.”

variant_text.sn46.76:4.2: Paṭikūlaanabhiratena → paṭikkūlānabhiratā (bj); paṭikūlena ca sabbaloke (sya-all); paṭikkūla anabhiratena (pts1ed)
